book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
To those who are to be Enlightened, delivered extempore at Jerusalem, as an Introductory Lecture to those who had come forward for Baptism.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: To those who are to be Enlightened, delivered extempore at Jerusalem, as an Introductory Lecture to those who had come forward for Baptism.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: FIRST CATECHETICAL LECTURE

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Our Holy Father Cyril,

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Archbishop of Jerusalem,

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: With a reading from Isaiah

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wash you, make you clean; put away your iniquities from your souls, from before mine eyes, and the rest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Disciples of the New Testament and partakers of the mysteries of Christ, as yet by calling only, but ere long by grace also, make you a new heart and a new spirit, that there may be gladness among the inhabitants of heaven: for if over one sinner that repenteth there is joy, according to the Gospel, how much more shall the salvation of so many souls move the inhabitants of heaven to gladness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: As ye have entered upon a good and most glorious path, run with reverence the race of godliness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Only-begotten Son of God is present here most ready to redeem you, saying, Come unto Me all that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ye that are clothed with the rough garment of your offences, who are holden with the cords of your own sins, hear the voice of the Prophet saying, Wash you, make you clean, put away your iniquities from before Mine eyes: that the choir of Angels may chant over you, Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven, and whose sins are covered.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ye who have just lighted the torches of faith, guard them carefully in your hands unquenched; that He, who erewhile on this all-holy Golgotha opened Paradise to the robber on account of his faith, may grant to you to sing the bridal song.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If any here is a slave of sin, let him promptly prepare himself through faith for the new birth into freedom and adoption; and having put off the miserable bondage of his sins, and taken on him the most blessed bondage of the Lord, so may he be counted worthy to inherit the kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Put off, by confession, the old man, which waxeth corrupt after the lusts of deceit, that ye may put on the new man, which is renewed according to knowledge of Him that created him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Get you the earnest of the Holy Spirit through faith, that ye may be able to be received into the everlasting habitations.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Come for the mystical Seal, that ye may be easily recognised by the Master; be ye numbered among the holy and spiritual flock of Christ, to be set apart on His right hand, and inherit the life prepared for you.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For they to whom the rough garment of their sins still clings are found on the left hand, because they came not to the grace of God which is given through Christ at the new birth of Baptism: new birth I mean not of bodies, but the spiritual new birth of the soul.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For our bodies are begotten by parents who are seen, but our souls are begotten anew through faith: for the Spirit bloweth where it listeth: and then, if thou be found worthy, thou mayest hear, Well done, good and faithful servant, when thou art found to have no defilement of hypocrisy in thy conscience.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if any of those who are present should think to tempt God’s grace, he deceives himself, and knows not its power.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Keep thy soul free from hypocrisy, O man, because of Him who searcheth hearts and reins.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For as those who are going to make a levy for war examine the ages and the bodies of those who are taking service, so also the Lord in enlisting souls examines their purpose: and if any has a secret hypocrisy, He rejects the man as unfit for His true service; but if He finds one worthy, to him He readily gives His grace.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He gives not holy things to the dogs; but where He discerns the good conscience, there He gives the Seal of salvation, that wondrous Seal, which devils tremble at, and Angels recognise; that the one may be driven to flight, and the others may watch around it as kindred to themselves.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Those therefore who receive this spiritual and saving Seal, have need also of the disposition akin to it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For as a writing-reed or a dart has need of one to use it, so grace also has need of believing minds.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou art receiving not a perishable but a spiritual shield.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Henceforth thou art planted in the invisible Paradise.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou receivest a new name, which thou hadst not before.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Heretofore thou wast a Catechumen, but now thou wilt be called a Believer.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou art transplanted henceforth among the spiritual olive-trees, being grafted from the wild into the good olive-tree, from sins into righteousness, from pollutions into purity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou art made partaker of the Holy Vine.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Well then, if thou abide in the Vine, thou growest as a fruitful branch; but if thou abide not, thou wilt be consumed by the fire.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us therefore bear fruit worthily.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God forbid that in us should be done what befell that barren fig-tree, that Jesus come not even now and curse us for our barrenness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But may all be able to use that other saying, But I am like a fruitful olive-tree in the house of God: I have trusted in the mercy of God for ever,—an olive-tree not to be perceived by sense, but by the mind, and full of light.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: As then it is His part to plant and to water, so it is thine to bear fruit: it is God’s to grant grace, but thine to receive and guard it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Despise not the grace because it is freely given, but receive and treasure it devoutly.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The present is the season of confession: confess what thou hast done in word or in deed, by night or by day; confess in an acceptable time, and in the day of salvation receive the heavenly treasure.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Devote thy time to the Exorcisms: be assiduous at the Catechisings, and remember the things that shall be spoken, for they are spoken not for thine ears only, but that by faith thou mayest seal them up in the memory.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Blot out from thy mind all earthly care: for thou art running for thy soul.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou art utterly forsaking the things of the world: little are the things which thou art forsaking, great what the Lord is giving.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Forsake things present, and put thy trust in things to come.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hast thou run so many circles of the years busied in vain about the world, and hast thou not forty days to be free (for prayer), for thine own soul’s sake?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be still, and know that I am God, saith the Scripture.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Excuse thyself from talking many idle words: neither backbite, nor lend a willing ear to backbiters; but rather be prompt to prayer.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Shew in ascetic exercise that thy heart is nerved.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Cleanse thy vessel, that thou mayest receive grace more abundantly.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For though remission of sins is given equally to all, the communion of the Holy Ghost is bestowed in proportion to each man’s faith.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou hast laboured little, thou receivest little; but if thou hast wrought much, the reward is great.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou art running for thyself, see to thine own interest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou hast aught against any man, forgive it: thou comest here to receive forgiveness of sins, and thou also must forgive him that hath sinned against thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Else with what face wilt thou say to the Lord, Forgive me my many sins, if thou hast not thyself forgiven thy fellow-servant even his little sins.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Attend diligently the Church assemblies; not only now when diligent attendance is required of thee by the Clergy, but also after thou hast received the grace.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if, before thou hast received it, the practice is good, is it not also good after the bestowal?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If before thou be grafted in, it is a safe course to be watered and tended, is it not far better after the planting?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wrestle for thine own soul, especially in such days as these.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nourish thy soul with sacred readings; for the Lord hath prepared for thee a spiritual table; therefore say thou also after the Psalmist, The Lord is my shepherd, and I shall lack nothing: in a place of grass, there hath He made me rest; He hath fed me beside the waters of comfort, He hath converted my soul:—that Angels also may share your joy, and Christ Himself the great High Priest, having accepted your resolve, may present you all to the Father, saying, Behold, I and the children whom God hath given Me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: May He keep you all well-pleasing in His sight!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: To whom be the glory, and the power unto the endless ages of eternity.
